questions.speaker: grandma
questions.sentence: Tonight's story has some new words. Let's learn them first!
questions.sentenceZh: 今晚的故事有幾個新字，我們先把它們學會！
questions.explanationZh: 奶奶說：今晚要講駱駝為什麼有駝峰，先來認識幾個關鍵字，記住了故事更好聽喔！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match each Chinese word to its English.
questions.explanationZh:
今晚故事裡的 4 個好字！
🔑 camel = 駱駝
🔑 desert = 沙漠
🔑 work = 工作
🔑 sand = 沙
把這幾個字記進心裡，故事聽起來就輕鬆多了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Four more words — can you match them all?
questions.explanationZh:
再來 4 個：
🔑 lazy = 懶
🔑 tired = 累
🔑 angry = 生氣
🔑 hump = 駝峰
「hump」就是駱駝背上那個大包，故事的名字就叫它！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Which one is a camel?
questions.question: Which one is a camel?
questions.explanationZh: camel = 駱駝 🐪！故事裡最懶的動物就是駱駝——他什麼都不做，只說「Humph！」

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Which one shows a desert?
questions.question: Which one shows a desert?
questions.explanationZh: desert = 沙漠 🏜️！故事裡的駱駝就住在廣闊沙漠的正中央，一個人都沒有。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Camel lived alone in the middle of the wide sand.
questions.sentenceZh: 駱駝獨自住在廣闊沙漠的正中央。
questions.question: Which picture matches the sentence?
options: a camel swimming in a big river
one lonely camel standing in a vast desert
a camel eating grass on a green hill
a camel walking through a snowy field
optionsZh: 一隻駱駝在大河裡游泳
一隻孤單的駱駝站在遼闊的沙漠
一隻駱駝在綠色山坡上吃草
一隻駱駝走過雪白的田野
questions.explanationZh: 故事說駱駝「獨自住在廣闊的沙漠正中央」——就是那隻一個人在沙漠裡的駱駝！

storyBeat: 三個夥伴去找他 — 他們要告狀嗎?

questions.speaker: grandma
questions.sentence: A few more words to learn — then the story begins!
questions.sentenceZh: 再記幾個字，故事就要開始了！
questions.explanationZh: 奶奶說：再學幾個重要的字，學會了，故事就更好懂囉！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match each Chinese phrase to its English.
questions.explanationZh:
這四個片語都在今晚的故事裡：
🔑 do nothing = 什麼都不做
🔑 work hard = 努力工作
🔑 flat back = 平平的背
🔑 wide sand = 廣闊的沙

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The other animals all worked hard from sun-up to sun-down.
questions.sentenceZh: 其他動物太陽剛升起就開始工作，一直忙到太陽落山。
questions.question: Which picture shows 'working hard'?
options: a horse pulling a heavy cart
an animal sleeping under a tree
a camel sitting and looking away
an ox standing still in the shade
optionsZh: 一隻駱駝坐著、把臉轉開
一隻牛站在陰影裡一動也不動
questions.explanationZh: work hard = 努力工作。故事裡馬、狗、牛都認真做事——拉重車就是努力工作的樣子！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Camel was very lazy and did nothing all day.
questions.question: Which emoji best shows 'lazy'?
questions.explanationZh: lazy = 懶。整天睡覺、什麼都不做——這就是「懶」的樣子！故事裡的駱駝就是這樣。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Camel ___ alone in the middle of the wide sand.
questions.sentenceHard: The Camel lived alone in the middle of the wide sand.
questions.question: Which word is correct?
questions.explanationZh: 這是「很久以前」發生的故事——駱駝「住在」沙漠裡要說「lived」！The Camel lived alone，不是 lives 或 living 喔。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Quick review — match all four words!
questions.explanationZh:
把 L1 和 L2 學過的字複習一遍！
🔑 hump = 駝峰
🔑 desert = 沙漠
🔑 lazy = 懶
🔑 tired = 累
記住這些，故事就好懂多了！

storyBeat: Man 訂規矩 — 沙塵裡誰來了?

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
再來 4 個字，學起來！
🔑 rule = 規矩
🔑 tired = 累
🔑 angry = 生氣
🔑 cloud = 雲
把這幾個字記住，待會的故事你會聽得很清楚。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: You can guess, my dear, where the three went to tell their story.
questions.explanationZh: 你猜得到嗎？三個夥伴氣沖沖地去找人類，把駱駝的事告訴他聽。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: They told Man that the Camel would not lift a finger.
questions.explanationZh: 他們說：駱駝連一根手指都不肯動！三個人說這話的時候，奶奶覺得一定都很委屈。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Man stood up at once and put both hands on his hips.
questions.questionEn: Was Man pleased with the news?
questions.explanationZh: 一下子站起來、雙手叉腰——這個動作你在家有沒有見過？這表示人類很不高興，根本不是很開心的樣子呢。
questions.sentenceZh: 人一下子就站起來，把雙手叉在腰上。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Man said, "Then you three must work double every day."
questions.explanationZh: 人說：「那你們三個，每天要做兩倍的工。」哇，本來就已經很累了，還要多做一倍！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: They had to do their own work and the Camel's work too.
questions.question: How would their days now feel?
questions.explanationZh: 自己的事做完，還要做駱駝不肯做的那份——時間根本不夠，當然是更長更累啦！
questions.sentenceZh: 他們得做自己的工作，還要做駱駝的那份。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: And so the three grew very tired and very, very angry.
questions.explanationZh: 就這樣，三個夥伴又累又生氣，越來越累、越來越生氣。奶奶說，這都是駱駝那一聲「Humph！」惹的禍。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Their backs hurt and they kicked the sand each evening.
questions.question: How did the three feel after many days?
questions.explanationZh: 背痛到踢沙子出氣——這哪裡是開心的樣子？又酸又痛又委屈，所以是「又酸痛又難過」。
questions.sentenceZh: 他們的背很痛，每天傍晚都在踢沙子出氣。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: One evening, a tall pillar of dust came rolling across the sand.
questions.explanationZh: 有一天傍晚，沙漠那頭出現了一根高高的沙柱，像一條龍，慢慢地滾過來……

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: What came toward them across the desert?
questions.question: What came toward them?
questions.explanationZh: 故事裡說有一根高高的沙柱滾過來——沙柱翻捲，就是沙塵雲啦！
questions.sentenceZh: 什麼東西朝他們穿越沙漠滾過來？

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: They told Man that the Camel would not lift a finger.
questions.question: Why did the three go to find Man?
options: asking for extra food and water
to ask for a shorter workday
optionsZh: 去告訴人類駱駝有多懶
給人類看他們的新把戲
questions.explanationZh: 三個夥伴說駱駝連一根手指都不肯動——他們去找人類，就是為了告狀、反映問題。
questions.sentenceZh: 他們告訴人類，駱駝連一根手指都不肯動。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match each phrase with its Chinese meaning.

pairs.left: put both hands on his hips

questions.explanationZh:
把這 4 個片語配一配！
🔑 put both hands on his hips = 雙手叉腰
🔑 work double = 做兩倍的工
🔑 from sun-up to sun-down = 從日出到日落
🔑 would not lift a finger = 連一根手指都不肯動

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Man said, "Then you three must work double every day."
questions.questionEn: Did Man excuse the three animals from their work?
questions.explanationZh: 人類說他們要做「兩倍」的工——這是加重負擔，根本沒有讓他們少做呀。
questions.sentenceZh: 人說：「那你們三個每天必須做兩倍的工。」

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: And so the three grew very tired and very, very angry.
questions.question: Which pair of emojis best shows how the three felt?
questions.explanationZh: 故事直接說了：他們又累又生氣（very tired and very angry）——就是又累又怒的樣子！
questions.sentenceZh: 就這樣，三個夥伴變得又非常累、又非常、非常生氣。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: And so the three animals grew very tired and very, very angry.
questions.questionEn: Were the three animals enjoying their extra work?
questions.explanationZh: 背痛、踢沙子出氣——這哪裡是享受的樣子？他們可痛苦了呢。
questions.sentenceZh: 他們的背很痛，每天傍晚都踢沙子出氣。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Man stood up at once and put both hands on his hips.
questions.question: What does Man's body language tell us?
options: he was angry and ready to act
the news made him feel sorry
his plan was to stay quiet
optionsZh: 他生氣了、準備採取行動
這個消息讓他感到難過
questions.explanationZh: 一下子站起來、雙手叉腰——這個姿勢表示人很不高興，準備要做點什麼了。
questions.sentenceZh: 人一下子就站起來，雙手叉在腰上。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match each phrase with its Chinese meaning.
questions.explanationZh:
再來 4 個！
🔑 grew very angry = 變得非常生氣
🔑 a tall pillar of dust = 一根高高的沙柱
🔑 rolled closer = 越滾越近
🔑 at once = 立刻、馬上

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Inside the tall pillar of dust, something was moving across the sand.
questions.questionEn: Was the dust a natural weather event?
questions.explanationZh: 故事後來告訴我們，那根沙柱裡面其實是精靈——這可不是普通的天氣現象喔！
questions.sentenceZh: 有一天傍晚，一根高高的沙柱橫越沙漠滾過來。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Man stood up at once and put both hands on his hips.
questions.question: Which picture best shows Man's reaction?
optionsZh: 雙手叉腰、看起來不高興
questions.explanationZh: 人一下子「站起來」「雙手叉腰」——這就是不高興又準備採取行動的樣子！
questions.sentenceZh: 人一下子就站起來，把雙手叉在腰上。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The dust rolled closer and closer, and inside it something moved.
questions.question: What does this clue tell us about what is coming?
options: something or someone special is arriving
a big sandstorm will bury the animals
the Camel is running away fast
food is falling from the sky
optionsZh: 有什麼特別的東西或人正在來
一場大沙暴要把動物埋起來
questions.explanationZh: 沙塵裡面有東西在動——這是個暗示，說明有什麼特別的東西（或某人）正在到來，故事要有新發展了。
questions.sentenceZh: 沙塵越滾越近，裡面有什麼東西在動……

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The dust rolled closer and closer, and inside it something moved...
questions.explanationZh: 沙塵越滾越近，越滾越近……裡面有什麼東西在動。奶奶說，是時候了。

storyBeat: Djinn 來了 — 他會幫誰?

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
今晚最重要的字來了！
🔑 djinn = 精靈
🔑 story = 故事
🔑 ask = 問
🔑 help = 幫忙
其中「djinn」是沙漠裡的魔法精靈，記住它！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Now, O dear listener, the dust opened and a Djinn stepped out.
questions.explanationZh: 你看！沙塵散開，走出來一個精靈——djinn！他穿越沙漠，來到三個疲累的動物面前。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He looked at the three tired animals with kind eyes.
questions.explanationZh: 他的眼神很親切，看著三個累壞了的動物，那樣溫柔的眼神，像是早就知道他們的委屈。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Djinn sat down low so he was the same height as the Dog.
questions.questionEn: Did the Djinn act in a friendly way?
questions.explanationZh: 你看，他特地蹲低身子，跟狗眼睛一樣高——這樣做，就是想讓動物們覺得他是自己人，很友善呢。
questions.sentenceZh: 精靈低下身，跟狗一樣高，然後坐下來。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Horse and Dog and Ox shared every part of their story.
questions.explanationZh: 馬、狗、牛三個把整件事從頭說到尾——駱駝怎麼不肯幫忙、他們怎麼做了雙倍的工，一點都沒有漏掉。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: They said one friend would not help and they did all his jobs.
questions.question: Why were they upset at the Camel?
questions.explanationZh: 那個朋友不肯動，別人的工作全堆到自己身上——這就是逃避工作嘛！
questions.sentenceZh: 他們說，有個朋友不肯幫忙，他們只好全部自己做。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Djinn rubbed his chin and thought for a long while.
questions.explanationZh: 精靈搓著下巴，想了好久好久。奶奶覺得，他心裡已經在想辦法了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He wanted to find out where this lazy friend was hiding.
questions.question: Why did the Djinn want to find that place?
options: bringing him a hot meal to eat
speaking with him face to face
questions.explanationZh: 精靈聽完這些，站起來想要找到那隻懶駱駝——當然是要去當面跟他說清楚啦！
questions.sentenceZh: 他想找出那個懶惰的朋友藏在哪裡。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "Where is this Camel?" the Djinn asked in a deep, soft voice.
questions.explanationZh: 「這隻駱駝在哪裡？」精靈用低沉又柔軟的聲音問，這樣的聲音讓奶奶覺得，有事情要發生了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: A new friend has come, has heard the three sad stories, and is ready to act.
questions.question: What is this scene mainly showing?
options: the Horse winning a big race in the desert
the Camel learning to dance in the sand
Man cooking dinner for the animals
the Djinn meeting the three and getting ready
optionsZh: 馬在沙漠贏了一場大比賽
精靈見到三隻動物、準備出手
questions.explanationZh: 精靈飛來、聽完委屈、開始動腦——這一段的重點，就是精靈見完三個夥伴之後，準備要去找駱駝算帳了。
questions.sentenceZh: 一個新朋友來了，聽完三個難過的故事，準備好要行動。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Now, O dear listener, the dust opened and a Djinn stepped out.
questions.question: What is special about how the Djinn arrived?
options: he came inside a cloud of dust
riding in on a white horse
stepping out from behind a tree
questions.explanationZh: 沙塵散開，精靈從裡面走出來——他的到來方式，就是從沙塵雲裡現身！（Djinn 精靈是阿拉伯故事裡的神靈，和歐洲的仙子不同——由無煙的火焰造成，能善能惡。）
questions.sentenceZh: 你看，親愛的聽眾，沙塵散開，一個精靈走了出來。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Djinn sat down low so he ___ the same height as the Dog.
questions.sentenceHard: The Djinn sat down low so he was the same height as the Dog.
questions.question: Which word is correct?
questions.explanationZh: 說故事裡的「he（他，一個人）」以前怎麼樣——英文用 was，不是 is、were 或 be 喔！
questions.sentenceZh: 精靈低低地坐下，讓自己跟狗一樣高。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match each phrase with its Chinese meaning.
questions.explanationZh:
把這 4 個片語配一配！
🔑 with kind eyes = 帶著親切的眼神
🔑 the same height as = 跟⋯⋯一樣高
🔑 rubbed his chin = 搓著下巴
🔑 face to face = 面對面

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He looked at the three tired animals with kind eyes.
questions.questionEn: Was the Djinn cold and harsh toward the three animals?
questions.explanationZh: 他用「親切的眼神」看著他們——完全不是冷漠或嚴厲的樣子呀。
questions.sentenceZh: 他帶著親切的眼神看著三隻疲累的動物。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Djinn rubbed his chin and thought for a long while.
questions.question: Which emoji best shows what the Djinn was doing?
questions.explanationZh: 搓著下巴、想了好久——這就是「認真在思考」的樣子！
questions.sentenceZh: 精靈搓著下巴，想了好久好久。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Horse and Dog and Ox shared every part of their story.
questions.questionEn: Did the three animals hide anything from the Djinn?
questions.explanationZh: 他們把整件事「從頭說到尾」——every part 就是每一個部分，什麼都說了，一點都沒有隱瞞喔。
questions.sentenceZh: 馬、狗、牛把自己的故事每一個部分都說了出來。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "Where is this Camel?" the Djinn asked in a deep, soft voice.
questions.question: What does the Djinn plan to do next?
options: fly away and leave the desert
ask Man to punish the Camel
give the three animals a long rest
questions.explanationZh: 精靈問「駱駝在哪裡？」——他問地點，就是準備自己去找他，要跟他說清楚。
questions.sentenceZh: 「這隻駱駝在哪裡？」精靈用低沉又柔軟的聲音問道。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match each phrase with its Chinese meaning.
questions.explanationZh:
配對！
🔑 stepped out = 走了出來
🔑 a deep soft voice = 低沉又柔軟的聲音
🔑 rose into the air = 飛上了天空
🔑 thought for a long while = 想了好久好久

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "I will go to him myself," the Djinn said, and rose into the air.
questions.questionEn: Did the Djinn send someone else to deal with the Camel?
questions.explanationZh: 他說「我自己去（I will go myself）」——這就是親自行動，沒有叫別人去呢。
questions.sentenceZh: 「我自己去找他，」精靈說，輕輕一躍，飛上了天空。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: A new friend has come, has heard the three sad stories, and is ready to act.
questions.question: Why is the Djinn a good person to solve this problem?
options: he listened carefully and has the power to help
his harsh words scared away the animals
he brought extra food for the team
he arrived too late to fix anything
optionsZh: 他仔細聽了、而且有能力幫忙
他嚴厲的話嚇走了動物們
他帶來了更多食物給大家
他來得太晚了、什麼都改變不了
questions.explanationZh: 精靈有親切的眼神、認真聽完了所有委屈、然後要親自去找駱駝——他仔細聽、又有能力，當然是解決問題的好人選！
questions.sentenceZh: 一個新的朋友來了，聽完三個難過的故事，準備好要行動了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "I will go to him myself," the Djinn said, and rose into the air...
questions.explanationZh: 「我自己去找他，」精靈說著，輕輕一躍，飛上了天空……奶奶說，駱駝啊，你的麻煩來了。

questions.sentence: The Djinn appeared.
questions.explanationZh: 「appeared」= 出現了。The Djinn appeared. 是最自然的說法。
questions.sentenceZh: 精靈出現了。

questions.sentence: He heard their story.
questions.explanationZh: 「heard」= 聽到了，「listened to」= 專心聽。兩個都對！
questions.sentenceZh: 他聽了他們的故事。

questions.sentence: The camel hid alone in the desert.
questions.answer: The camel hid alone in the desert.
accept: The camel was alone in the desert.
The camel stayed alone in the desert.
The camel hid by himself in the desert.
questions.explanationZh: 「hid」= 躲了起來——說發生過的事，hide 要變成 hid。「alone」= 一個人，一個字就夠了！
questions.sentenceZh: 駱駝一個人藏在沙漠裡。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Djinn heard the three animals and rubbed his chin for a long time before he asked where the Camel was.
questions.question: Why did the Djinn pause before asking about the Camel?
options: He needed time to decide what to do.
He wanted the animals to do it themselves.
He was too tired to speak right away.
optionsZh: 他需要時間想清楚怎麼做
他想讓動物們自己去處理
questions.explanationZh: 精靈搓了好久的下巴——這個動作代表他在認真思考。先想清楚，再行動，才是有智慧的做法。

storyBeat: Djinn 警告 — 是什麼魔法?

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
又來 4 個好字！
🔑 friend = 朋友
🔑 hear = 聽見
🔑 sit down = 坐下
🔑 magic = 魔法
最後那個字最重要——magic，魔法！等一下你就知道為什麼。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: You see, my dear, the Djinn flew to the Howling Desert.
questions.explanationZh: 你看，精靈飛啊飛，飛到了一個叫做 Howling Desert 的地方——就是那個會嚎叫的大沙漠。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Camel sat under a thorn tree, doing nothing at all.
questions.explanationZh: 駱駝坐在一棵有刺的樹下，什麼都沒做。奶奶說，他大概還以為今天跟往常一樣，什麼事都不會發生。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Djinn watched him for many slow, quiet minutes.
questions.questionEn: Did the Djinn act in a rushed way?
questions.explanationZh: 「好幾分鐘」、「靜靜地」——精靈不急不躁，慢慢看，慢慢想，這根本不是著急的樣子呢。
questions.sentenceZh: 精靈靜靜地看著他，看了好幾分鐘。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Djinn said, "My friend, I have heard a sad report."
questions.explanationZh: 精靈開口了：「我的朋友，我聽到了一個很難過的消息。」他說話很輕，但駱駝一定聽到了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He spoke about a friend who would not lift his share.
questions.question: Who ended up paying for that choice?
questions.explanationZh: 那個不肯做工的傢伙——他的那份工作，不就全堆到別人身上了嗎？所以是「其他動物們」替他扛。
questions.sentenceZh: 他說到有個朋友不肯做自己那份工作。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Camel only said his one rude word once more: "Humph!"
questions.explanationZh: 駱駝聽完，又再說了那個字：「Humph！」這一次，精靈沒有走掉。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He chose to stick with the same lazy reply once again.
questions.question: How did the Camel choose to act?
questions.explanationZh: 他又是那個字，還是「Humph！」——一個字都沒改，同樣粗魯，同樣把人家推走。
questions.sentenceZh: 他選擇再一次用同樣懶懶的方式回答。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Djinn sat down and placed his chin upon his hand.
questions.explanationZh: 精靈坐下來，把下巴擱在手上——你看，這個姿勢，奶奶一看就知道，有事要發生了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: What was the Djinn about to do?
questions.question: What was he about to do?
questions.explanationZh: 坐下來、下巴擱在手上——這是在凝神準備施法的姿勢喔！所以是要施魔法。
questions.sentenceZh: 精靈準備要做什麼？

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: You see, my dear, the Djinn flew to the Howling Desert.
questions.question: Where did the Djinn go to find the Camel?
options: into a green forest far away
optionsZh: 一座白雪皚皚的高山上
遠方一片綠色的森林裡
questions.explanationZh: 故事說精靈飛到了「Howling Desert（嚎叫沙漠，這是吉卜林虛構的地名，不是真實地點）」——那就是他去找駱駝的地方。『You see, my dear』是英國作家吉卜林說故事的口頭禪，他會像奶奶一樣直接對你說話，今晚奶奶學他！
questions.sentenceZh: 你看，親愛的，精靈飛到了嚎叫的沙漠。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match each phrase with its Chinese meaning.
questions.explanationZh:
再來 4 個片語！
🔑 doing nothing at all = 什麼都沒在做
🔑 a sad report = 一個難過的消息
🔑 lift his share = 做自己那份工
🔑 sat under a thorn tree = 坐在一棵有刺的樹下

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Camel sat under a thorn tree, doing nothing at all.
questions.questionEn: Was the Camel busy working when the Djinn found him?
questions.explanationZh: 故事說駱駝坐在那裡，「什麼都沒做（doing nothing at all）」——他根本沒在工作呀。
questions.sentenceZh: 駱駝坐在一棵有刺的樹下，什麼都沒在做。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Djinn watched him for many slow, quiet minutes.
questions.question: Which emoji best shows how the Djinn watched the Camel?
questions.explanationZh: 「好幾分鐘、靜靜地」——精靈不急不躁，就靜靜地看著他，這就是平靜有耐心的樣子。
questions.sentenceZh: 精靈靜靜地看著他，看了好幾分鐘。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Djinn sat down and placed his chin upon his hand.
questions.questionEn: Did the Camel change his behavior after hearing the Djinn's warning?
questions.explanationZh: 精靈說完之後，駱駝還是說「Humph！」——他一點都沒有改變呢。
questions.sentenceZh: 駱駝又再說了他那個粗魯的字：「Humph！」

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He spoke about a friend who would not lift his share.
questions.question: Why did the Djinn come to tell the Camel this?
options: bringing the Camel a warm meal
teaching the Camel a new song
to give the Camel a chance to change
optionsZh: 給駱駝一個改變的機會
questions.explanationZh: 精靈先好好跟他說了其他動物的困境——這是先給他一個機會，讓他自己想清楚、改變。
questions.sentenceZh: 他說了一個不肯做自己那份工的朋友的事。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match each phrase with its Chinese meaning.

pairs.left: placed his chin upon his hand

questions.explanationZh:
配對！
🔑 placed his chin upon his hand = 把下巴擱在手上
🔑 a great Magic = 一個大魔法
🔑 very slowly = 非常慢慢地
🔑 once more = 又再一次

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He chose to stick with the same lazy reply once again.
questions.questionEn: Did the Camel try to make things better?
questions.explanationZh: 他選擇「繼續用同樣懶懶的回答」——什麼改變都沒有，完全沒有想讓事情變好的意思喔。
questions.sentenceZh: 他選擇再一次堅持同樣懶懶的回答。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Djinn sat down and placed his chin upon his hand.
questions.question: Which picture best shows what the Djinn did?
questions.explanationZh: 精靈坐下來，把下巴擱在手上——這就是沉思、思考的姿勢！
questions.sentenceZh: 精靈坐下來，把下巴擱在手上。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Then, very slowly, the Djinn began to make a great Magic.
questions.question: What shows that something big is about to happen?
options: the Djinn begins making magic slowly
the Camel starts working at once
optionsZh: 精靈開始慢慢地施起魔法
questions.explanationZh: 精靈開始施起「一個大大的魔法（a great Magic）」——這就是在暗示，大事要來了！
questions.sentenceZh: 然後，非常非常慢地，精靈開始施起一個大魔法……

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Then, very slowly, the Djinn began to make a great Magic...
questions.explanationZh: 然後，慢慢地、慢慢地，精靈開始施起一個大大的魔法……奶奶說，駱駝的背，要開始有故事了。

storyBeat: 駝峰出現 — 背還會停嗎?

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
今晚 4 個字很重要，把它們記進去！
🔑 flat = 平的
🔑 back = 背
🔑 behind = 後面
🔑 hump = 駝峰
最後那個——hump，駝峰——就是今天故事的主角！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Now watch, my dear, what happened next under that hot sun.
questions.explanationZh: 奶奶說，仔細瞧瞧，那烈日下，接下來發生的事，可是很神奇的哦。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Camel's back was flat and smooth, like a wide soft mat.
questions.explanationZh: 你看，駱駝的背本來平平的、滑滑的，像一塊軟軟的大墊子。沒有一點突起，連個小山丘都沒有。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: You could place a small cup on his back and it would not slide.
questions.questionEn: Did the Camel already have a hump?
questions.explanationZh: 背這麼平，杯子放上去都不會滑——這就說明，駱駝那時候的背完全平平的，根本還沒有駝峰嘛！
questions.sentenceZh: 你可以在他背上放一個小杯子，它也不會滑掉。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Camel said "Humph!" one more time, just to be rude.
questions.explanationZh: 駱駝又說了一次「Humph！」——就這樣，只是因為他懶得說別的。奶奶說，這一次，這個字說出口，有點不一樣了……

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: And as he said that word, his flat back began to push up.
questions.question: What does this tell us about his rude word?
optionsZh: 一股改變他身體的力量
讓太陽變得更熱的東西
在呼喚其他動物的信號
questions.explanationZh: 他一說「Humph！」，背就開始變形——那個字，就像一道咒語，讓他的身體開始改變了！
questions.sentenceZh: 就在他說出那個字的同時，他平平的背開始往上拱。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: It puffed up and puffed up and puffed up some more.
questions.explanationZh: 它鼓起來、又鼓起來、再鼓起來……就像一個麵包在烤箱裡慢慢膨脹，停都停不下來。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: When he turned his head, a great soft shape was rising up behind.
questions.question: What did the Camel see behind him?
options: a new bump on his back
optionsZh: 背上出現一個新的隆起
兩隻小鳥飛來停在上面
questions.explanationZh: 他轉頭一看，背後一大塊軟軟的東西正在長起來——背上出現了一個新的隆起！
questions.sentenceZh: 他回頭一看，背後一個又大又軟的形狀慢慢升起來。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: It was, my dear, his very own big hump — and it stayed.
questions.explanationZh: 親愛的，那就是他自己的大駝峰——它長出來了，而且再也不會消失了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The soft shape stayed on him, and it did not go down again.
questions.question: What might this new shape mean for his future?
options: he can keep saying humph and stay free
Man will let him sleep all year
the other animals will give him gifts
he will carry something every single day
optionsZh: 他可以繼續說 humph，保持自由
其他動物會來送他禮物
他以後每天都要帶著這個東西
questions.explanationZh: 駝峰長出來、不會消失——這意思就是，以後不管到哪裡，他的背上都要帶著這個了。奶奶說，這可是他自己說出來的呢。
questions.sentenceZh: 那個軟軟的形狀就這樣留在他身上，再也沒有縮回去。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Camel's back was flat and smooth, like a wide soft mat.
questions.question: What does this description tell us about the Camel before the magic?
options: his back was covered in spikes
his back hurt all the time
questions.explanationZh: 平平的、滑滑的、像墊子——這就是說他的背完全沒有突起，還沒有駝峰！
questions.sentenceZh: 駱駝的背平平的、光滑的，像一塊寬大的軟墊。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match each phrase with its Chinese meaning.
questions.explanationZh:
把這 4 個片語配一配！
🔑 flat and smooth = 平平的、光滑的
🔑 began to push up = 開始往上鼓
🔑 puffed up = 膨脹起來
🔑 rising up behind = 在後面升起來

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: It puffed up and puffed up and puffed up some more above his back.
questions.questionEn: Did the hump appear before the Camel said "Humph!"?
questions.explanationZh: 故事說「就在他說出那個字的同時（as he said that word）」，背才開始鼓起來——是說完之後才出現的，不是之前喔。
questions.sentenceZh: 就在他說出那個字的同時，他平平的背開始往上鼓。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: It puffed up and puffed up and puffed up some more.
questions.question: Which emoji best shows what happened to the Camel's back?
questions.explanationZh: 「鼓起來、又鼓起來、再鼓起來」——一直變大、一直長，所以是越來越大！
questions.sentenceZh: 它鼓起來、又鼓起來、再鼓起來。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: It was, my dear, his very own big hump — and it stayed.
questions.questionEn: Did the hump go away after a while?
questions.explanationZh: 故事說「it stayed（它留下來了）」——駝峰長出來就不走了，永遠留在背上呢。
questions.sentenceZh: 那就是，親愛的，他自己的大駝峰——而且它留下來了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Camel said "Humph!" one more time, just to be rude.
questions.question: What does "just to be rude" tell us about the Camel?
options: too scared to speak at all
trying to be kind but failing
he was rude on purpose, not by accident
wanting to sing but forgot the words
optionsZh: 嘗試表現親切但沒成功
他是故意粗魯的，不是意外
questions.explanationZh: 「just to be rude（只是為了粗魯）」——這說得很清楚，他完全是故意的，不是不小心。
questions.sentenceZh: 駱駝又說了一次「Humph！」，就只是為了粗魯。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match each phrase with its Chinese meaning.
questions.explanationZh:
再配對！
🔑 his very own big hump = 他自己的大駝峰
🔑 a great soft shape = 一個大大的軟形狀
🔑 turned his head = 轉過頭去
🔑 it stayed = 它留下來了

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: When he turned his head, a great soft shape was rising up behind.
questions.questionEn: Did the Camel notice his hump right away?
questions.explanationZh: 他「轉過頭去」才看到——他發現了自己背後的變化，確實注意到了喔。
questions.sentenceZh: 他回頭一看，背後一個大大的軟形狀正在升起來。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Camel's back was flat and smooth, like a wide soft mat.
questions.question: Which shows the Camel BEFORE the magic?
options: wearing a big bag on its back
one with a huge tall hump already
one with two humps on its back
a camel with a flat smooth back and no hump
optionsZh: 背上背著一個大袋子的駱駝
一隻已經有高大駝峰的駱駝
一隻背上有兩個駝峰的駱駝
一隻背部平整、沒有駝峰的駱駝
questions.explanationZh: 在魔法發生之前，駱駝的背是「平平的、光滑的，像墊子」——完全沒有任何駝峰！
questions.sentenceZh: 駱駝的背平平的、光滑的，像一塊寬大的軟墊。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The soft shape stayed on him, and it did not go down again.
questions.question: What does this tell us about the Djinn's magic?
options: fixed with just a bit of food
optionsZh: 只需要一點食物就能修好
questions.explanationZh: 軟形狀留下來了、再也沒有縮回去——這就說明精靈的魔法是永久的，不會消失的。
questions.sentenceZh: 那個軟形狀就這樣留在他身上，再也沒有縮回去。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: And the great hump rose higher and higher above his back...
questions.explanationZh: 那個大駝峰，越長越高、越長越高……奶奶說，下一晚，精靈要來說話了。

storyBeat: 教訓 — 你背上的是什麼?

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
最後一課，再記 4 個字！
🔑 own = 自己的
🔑 bring = 帶來
🔑 three days = 三天
🔑 forget = 忘記
學完這 4 個，駱駝的故事就圓滿了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Then the Djinn turned, my dear, and spoke clear and slow.
questions.explanationZh: 然後，精靈轉過身，慢慢地、清清楚楚地說話了。奶奶屏著呼吸等——他要說什麼呢？

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He said, "That hump is what you brought upon yourself."
questions.explanationZh: 他說：「那個駝峰，是你自己帶到自己身上的。」聽到這話，奶奶覺得，這就是最公平的答案了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Camel hung his head and looked at the sand for a long time.
questions.questionEn: Did the Camel feel proud of his new hump?
questions.explanationZh: 低著頭，看著腳下的沙，看了好久——這哪裡是驕傲的樣子？他是難過的，覺得慚愧的呀。
questions.sentenceZh: 駱駝低下頭，看著沙地，看了好久好久。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Djinn said, "Now you can work three days with no food."
questions.explanationZh: 精靈繼續說：「現在好了，你可以三天不吃東西，也能工作了。」駝峰，原來是有用的。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The big shape stored what he needed to live on the road.
questions.question: Why was that helpful for the Camel?
options: winning every race in the desert
optionsZh: 在沙漠贏得每一場賽跑
走很遠的路不用停下來
questions.explanationZh: 駝峰把食物和水分存起來——這樣走長長的路，就不必一直停下來找吃的了。
questions.sentenceZh: 那個大形狀把他路上需要的東西都存起來了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: And so, my dear, the Camel went off to find the other three.
questions.explanationZh: 就這樣，親愛的，駱駝去找那三個等了他很久的夥伴。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: From that day, he carried bags across the sand with them.
questions.question: How did the Camel join the team at last?
questions.explanationZh: 他跟著大家，一起扛袋子穿沙漠——這就是「搬運東西」，終於加入了隊伍。
questions.sentenceZh: 從那天起，他跟他們一起扛著袋子穿越沙漠。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He still has that hump today, my dear, so he will never forget.
questions.explanationZh: 他到今天還帶著那個駝峰，親愛的——奶奶說，那就是為了讓他永遠記得這件事。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The thing he tried to skip became the thing he carries every day.
questions.question: What does the hump teach us, my dear?
options: magic is the best way to fix things
all camels are born with humps
work you skip can come back heavier
optionsZh: 逃避工作會讓生活更輕鬆
魔法是解決問題最好的方法
所有駱駝天生就有駝峰
你逃掉的工作，最後會變成你的包袱
questions.explanationZh: 他不肯做的那份工，最後變成了背上的駝峰，天天跟著他——奶奶說，這個故事告訴我們，逃掉的事情，不會真的消失的。
questions.sentenceZh: 他本來想逃掉的那件事，變成了他每天都要帶著的東西。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He said, "That hump is what you brought upon yourself."
questions.question: What is the Djinn saying about the hump?
options: the Camel caused it by his own laziness
the other animals put it there
Man asked the Djinn to add it
it fell from the sky by accident
optionsZh: 駱駝因為自己的懶惰才長出來的
其他動物把它放上去的
人類要求精靈加上去的
從天上掉下來的，是意外
questions.explanationZh: 「你自己帶到自己身上的（what you brought upon yourself）」——這就是說，是駱駝的懶惰和不禮貌造成的，完全是他自己的責任。
questions.sentenceZh: 他說：「那個駝峰，是你自己帶到自己身上的。」

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match each phrase with its Chinese meaning.
questions.explanationZh:
把這 4 個片語配一配！
🔑 brought upon yourself = 自己帶給自己的
🔑 hung his head = 低下頭
🔑 speak clear and slow = 說得清楚又慢
🔑 three days with no food = 三天不吃東西

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Djinn said, "Now you can work three days with no food."
questions.questionEn: Did the hump turn out to be completely useless?
questions.explanationZh: 精靈說駝峰讓他可以三天不吃東西也能工作——這是有用的！一點也不是毫無用處喔。
questions.sentenceZh: 精靈說：「現在你可以三天不吃東西，也能工作了。」

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Camel hung his head and looked at the sand for a long time.
questions.question: Which emoji best shows how the Camel felt?
questions.explanationZh: 低著頭、看著沙地、看了好久——這就是羞愧、難過、沒話說的樣子。
questions.sentenceZh: 駱駝低下頭，看著沙地，看了好久好久。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: From that day, he carried bags across the sand with them.
questions.questionEn: Did the Camel continue to do nothing after getting his hump?
questions.explanationZh: 從那天起，他就跟大家一起扛袋子了——他終於開始工作，不再什麼都不做了呢。
questions.sentenceZh: 從那天起，他跟他們一起扛著袋子穿越沙漠。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He still has that hump today, my dear, so he will never forget.
questions.question: Why does the Camel still have his hump today?
options: Man asked him to keep it
as a reminder of his lazy past
the Djinn could not remove it
optionsZh: 因為它看起來非常漂亮
作為提醒他懶惰過去的記號
精靈沒有辦法把它拿走
questions.explanationZh: 故事說他帶著駝峰，「所以他永遠不會忘記」——駝峰是一個提醒，讓他記住不努力工作的後果。
questions.sentenceZh: 他今天還帶著那個駝峰，親愛的，這樣他就永遠不會忘記了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match each phrase with its Chinese meaning.

pairs.right: 把他需要的東西存起來

questions.explanationZh:
再來 4 個片語！
🔑 carried bags across the sand = 扛著袋子穿越沙漠
🔑 went off to find = 去找……
🔑 never forget = 永遠不會忘記
🔑 stored what he needed = 把他需要的東西存起來

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The big shape stored what he needed to live on the road.
questions.questionEn: Did the hump help the Camel survive long journeys?
questions.explanationZh: 駝峰把路上需要的東西存起來——這讓他可以走長長的路，不用停下來找吃的，當然有幫到他！
questions.sentenceZh: 那個大形狀把他路上需要的東西都存起來了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: From that day, he carried bags across the sand with them.
questions.question: Which picture shows the Camel at the END of the story?
options: eating happily with no hump at all
sitting alone under a tree doing nothing
running away from the other animals
a camel carrying bags in the desert with friends
optionsZh: 開心地吃東西、完全沒有駝峰
獨自坐在樹下什麼都不做
一隻駱駝和朋友們在沙漠裡扛袋子
questions.explanationZh: 故事結尾說，從那天起他跟大家一起扛袋子穿越沙漠——所以他最後是加入了團隊，一起工作的！
questions.sentenceZh: 從那天起，他跟他們一起扛著袋子穿越沙漠。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The thing he tried to skip became the thing he carries every day.
questions.question: What is the main lesson of this whole story?
options: avoiding your duty leads to a heavier burden
magic is the only way to fix laziness
deserts are too hot to work in
optionsZh: 逃避責任，最後反而要背更重的包袱
魔法是修正懶惰的唯一方法
沙漠太熱了，根本沒法工作
questions.explanationZh: 駱駝逃掉的那份工，最後變成了他背上的駝峰，天天帶著走——這個故事告訴我們，逃避責任不會讓事情消失，反而讓你的負擔更重。
questions.sentenceZh: 他本來想逃掉的那件事，變成了他每天都要帶著的東西。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: And you, my dear — what hump might you be carrying tonight?
questions.explanationZh: 而你，親愛的——奶奶輕聲問，你背上，今晚有什麼還沒做完的事嗎？
